info.name: Aptitude Manual QA
info.description:
Rerunnable Postman collection for manual QA of the Aptitude registry API.

Run folders in order for the full end-to-end flow:
  1. 00 Operability
  2. 10 Publish And Exact Reads
  3. 20 Lifecycle And Access Policy
  4. 30 Trust Tier Policy
  5. 40 Auth And Validation

The collection seeds a unique run id automatically and derives unique slugs from it, so reruns do not require a clean database. Environment variables only need base URL and tokens.

header.description: Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect a JSON response body.
Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect a JSON response body.

exec: pm.test('Readyz payload reports database readiness', () => { pm.expect(json.status).to.eql('ready'); pm.expect(json.checks).to.be.an('array').and.not.empty; pm.expect(json.checks.some((check) => check.name === 'database' && check.status === 'ok')).to.eql(true); });

header.description: Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect Prometheus text exposition.

exec: pm.test('Metrics endpoint exposes Prometheus text payload', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.headers.get('Content-Type')).to.include('text/plain'); pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.include('aptitude_http_requests_total'); pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.include('aptitude_database_ready'); });

header.description: Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect a JSON response body.
Send a JSON request body.
Authenticate with a caller that has the `publish` scope.
Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect a JSON response body.
Send a JSON request body.
Authenticate with a caller that has the `publish` scope.

exec: pm.test('Primary v1 publish returns internal provenance metadata', () => { pm.expect(json.slug).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primarySlug')); pm.expect(json.version).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primaryV1')); pm.expect(json.trust_tier).to.eql('internal'); pm.expect(json.provenance.repo_url).to.eql('https://github.com/example/skills'); pm.expect(json.provenance.publisher_identity).to.eql('postman/manual-qa'); pm.expect(json.provenance.trust_context.trust_tier).to.eql('internal'); });

header.description: Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect a JSON response body.
Send a JSON request body.
Authenticate with a caller that has the `publish` scope.

exec: pm.test('Primary v2 publish returns immutable metadata', () => { pm.expect(json.slug).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primarySlug')); pm.expect(json.version).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primaryV2')); pm.expect(json.lifecycle_status).to.eql('published'); pm.expect(json.trust_tier).to.eql('internal'); });

header.description: Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect a JSON response body.
Authenticate with a caller that has the `read` scope.

exec: pm.test('Primary metadata matches the current contract', () => { pm.expect(json.slug).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primarySlug')); pm.expect(json.version).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primaryV2')); pm.expect(json.lifecycle_status).to.eql('published'); pm.expect(json.trust_tier).to.eql('internal'); pm.expect(json.metadata.name).to.eql('Postman QA Primary'); pm.expect(json.metadata.tags).to.include('primary'); pm.expect(json.provenance.trust_context.policy_profile).to.eql('default'); });

header.description: Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect the immutable markdown body.
Authenticate with a caller that has the `read` scope.

exec: pm.test('Primary content returns immutable markdown with cache headers', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.headers.get('Content-Type')).to.include('text/markdown'); pm.expect(pm.response.headers.get('ETag')).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primaryV2Digest')); pm.expect(pm.response.headers.get('Cache-Control')).to.eql('public, immutable'); pm.expect(pm.response.headers.get('Content-Length')).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primaryV2Size')); pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.include('Run `' + runId + '` primary v2'); });

header.description: Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect a JSON response body.
Authenticate with a caller that has the `read` scope.

exec: pm.test('Resolution returns the authored direct dependency selectors only', () => { pm.expect(json.slug).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primarySlug')); pm.expect(json.version).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primaryV2')); pm.expect(json.depends_on).to.have.length(1); pm.expect(json.depends_on[0].slug).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('dependencySlug')); pm.expect(json.depends_on[0].version).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primaryV1')); pm.expect(json.depends_on[0].version_constraint).to.eql(null); pm.expect(json.depends_on[0].optional).to.eql(false); pm.expect(json.depends_on[0].markers).to.eql(['linux']); });

header.description: Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect a JSON response body.
Send a JSON request body.
Authenticate with a caller that has the `read` scope.

exec: pm.test('Discovery returns the primary slug as a candidate', () => { pm.expect(json.candidates).to.include(pm.collectionVariables.get('primarySlug')); });

header.description: Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect a JSON response body.

exec: pm.test('Deprecated metadata remains readable to readers', () => { pm.expect(json.lifecycle_status).to.eql('deprecated'); pm.expect(json.slug).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primarySlug')); });

header.description: Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect a JSON response body.
Authenticate with a caller that has the `read` scope.

exec: pm.test('Archived metadata is denied to readers', () => { pm.expect(json.error.code).to.eql('POLICY_EXACT_READ_FORBIDDEN'); });

header.description: Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.
Expect a JSON response body.
Authenticate with a caller that has the `admin` scope.

exec: pm.test('Archived metadata remains readable to admins', () => { pm.expect(json.slug).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primarySlug')); pm.expect(json.version).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primaryV1')); pm.expect(json.lifecycle_status).to.eql('archived'); });

exec: pm.test('Archived content is denied to readers', () => { pm.expect(json.error.code).to.eql('POLICY_EXACT_READ_FORBIDDEN'); });

header.description: Correlate the request through logs, metrics, and audit events.

exec: pm.test('Archived content remains readable to admins', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.headers.get('ETag')).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primaryV1Digest')); pm.expect(pm.response.headers.get('Content-Length')).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primaryV1Size')); pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.include('Run `' + runId + '` primary v1'); });

exec: pm.test('Archived resolution is denied to readers', () => { pm.expect(json.error.code).to.eql('POLICY_EXACT_READ_FORBIDDEN'); });

exec: pm.test('Archived resolution remains readable to admins', () => { pm.expect(json.slug).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primarySlug')); pm.expect(json.version).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('primaryV1')); });
